project.name: bot-store-management
project.description: A Playwright-based automation bot for managing ecommerce orders, supporting order collection with tag/address/product enrichment and batch order confirmation workflows.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init that re-exports core symbols from auth, config_loader, login, order_page, rules, and store modules for convenient top-level imports.

nodes.name: auth.py
nodes.summary: Bearer token extraction from browser storage (localStorage, sessionStorage, IndexedDB), JWT parsing, token expiry checking, and saving/loading auth tokens to disk. Provides capture_and_save_auth_token as the main entry point.

nodes.name: _extract_bearer_from_value
nodes.summary: Recursively extracts a JWT bearer token string from a raw string, dict, or list value by checking known token key names.

nodes.name: _extract_token_metadata
nodes.summary: Normalizes and extracts token metadata (token_type, expires_in, issued_at, expires_at, has_refresh_token) from a raw auth payload.

nodes.name: _is_saved_token_expired
nodes.summary: Checks whether a saved token has expired using multiple expiry resolution strategies.

nodes.name: _seed_token_in_browser_storage
nodes.summary: Seeds a saved bearer token into the browser's localStorage, sessionStorage, and all IndexedDB databases via Playwright page.evaluate, enabling auto-login bypass.

nodes.name: _capture_bearer_from_indexeddb
nodes.summary: Scans all available IndexedDB databases/stores for a token key via Playwright evaluate, returning the first found bearer token with its source info.

nodes.name: capture_bearer_token
nodes.summary: Captures a bearer token from the browser's localStorage/sessionStorage first, then falls back to IndexedDB scan.

nodes.name: save_auth_token
nodes.summary: Persists a captured bearer token with metadata to the configured token file path on disk.

nodes.name: capture_and_save_auth_token
nodes.summary: Orchestrates token capture (with dashboard URL fallback) and saves to disk; main public entry point for token capture flow.

nodes.name: bot_config.py
nodes.summary: Typed accessor class BotConfig wrapping the config dict with named properties for bot settings, feature flags, Playwright timeouts, and message templates. Validates required keys at startup.

nodes.name: BotConfig
nodes.summary: Typed config accessor with startup validation of all required timeout and message keys. Exposes properties for bot limits, feature flags, Playwright timeouts, and Vietnamese message templates.

nodes.name: cli_helpers.py
nodes.summary: CLI prompting helpers for feature selection, campaign date resolution, price code mapping, and existing CSV file selection using InquirerPy menus.

nodes.name: resolve_campaign_date
nodes.summary: Parses various date input formats into a canonical d/m/yyyy string for campaign filtering.

nodes.name: prompt_campaign_label
nodes.summary: Interactive prompt that lets the user pick yesterday, today, or a custom date as the campaign label string.

nodes.name: prompt_price_code_mapping
nodes.summary: Prompts user to enter the number of active A-codes and their prices, returning a dict mapping code to integer price or None.

nodes.name: prompt_existing_csv_required
nodes.summary: Lists existing orders CSV files in the data directory and prompts user to select one.

nodes.name: cli_menu.py
nodes.summary: ANSI terminal UI module using InquirerPy for in-place step-by-step rendering: banner, arrow-key select menus, text input, and boxed summary display.

nodes.name: select
nodes.summary: Renders an arrow-key select menu with step indicator header, clears previous output, and prints a permanent done line with the chosen value.

nodes.name: show_summary
nodes.summary: Prints a boxed ANSI summary of all selected options with aligned labels and values.

nodes.name: config_loader.py
nodes.summary: YAML config loading with PyYAML primary and hand-rolled fallback parser. Validates required keys and normalises all config sections into a canonical dict for runtime use.

nodes.name: _safe_load_yaml
nodes.summary: Attempts yaml.safe_load; falls back to a custom line-by-line YAML parser.

nodes.name: load_config
nodes.summary: Loads and validates config.yaml, normalises all sections into a canonical dict. Raises on missing base_url.

nodes.name: constants.py
nodes.summary: Defines the full tag system (TAG_0 through TAG_2_4, ERR), internal status name constants, STATUS_TO_TAG mapping, and tag sets RECHECK_TAGS, OOS_TAGS, TAG_ONLY_TAGS, ORDER_TAG_VALUES.

nodes.name: login.py
nodes.summary: Authentication flow: token bootstrap, auto-login (form fill with multiple selector fallbacks), manual login wait, browser context creation with session persistence and bad-session quarantine.

nodes.name: try_auto_login
nodes.summary: Attempts automated login by finding username/password inputs and submit button via multiple selector fallbacks.

nodes.name: try_login_with_saved_token
nodes.summary: Loads saved token from disk, seeds it into browser storage, navigates to dashboard, and returns whether the login page is bypassed.

nodes.name: ensure_login
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full login flow: saved token → auto-login → manual login wait. Saves session state on success.

nodes.name: new_context
nodes.summary: Creates a Playwright browser context, restoring session from file if valid, quarantining invalid session files and falling back to fresh context.

nodes.name: note_parser.py
nodes.summary: Extracts price tokens from Vietnamese ecommerce order note text. Handles A-code aliases, phone number exclusion, time/weight pattern exclusion, and per-line ambiguity resolution.

nodes.name: extract_note_prices
nodes.summary: Main exported function. Iterates lines of order note text, resolves A-code aliases via mapping, removes phone patterns, and extracts exactly one price per unambiguous line.

nodes.name: _find_a_code_price
nodes.summary: Checks if a line contains an A-code (A1-A9, optional space) and returns its mapped price.

nodes.name: _build_code_pattern
nodes.summary: Builds a regex pattern for an A-code allowing optional space between letter and digit and various valid suffixes.

nodes.name: order_page.py
nodes.summary: Page Object Model for all Playwright interactions with the TPOS order management UI. Handles selectors, pagination, row extraction, modal interactions, message/image sending, tag application, product image download, and the two-pass order enrichment workflow (2310 lines).

nodes.name: OrderPage
nodes.summary: Central Page Object Model class encapsulating all browser automation logic.

nodes.name: collect_and_enrich_single_pass
nodes.summary: Single-pass workflow: iterates all paginated rows, immediately enriches each qualifying order, and writes results to CSV writer.

nodes.name: enrich_collected_rows
nodes.summary: Two-pass second stage: takes pre-collected rows, iterates pages to find each, opens modal, evaluates address/products, applies tags, sends messages.

nodes.name: read_filtered_orders
nodes.summary: First pass of two-pass collect: iterates all paginated rows and collects basic order data without opening modals.

nodes.name: find_row_by_code_paginated
nodes.summary: Searches paginated order table for a row matching a specific order code.

nodes.name: _evaluate_modal_address_and_product
nodes.summary: Opens the order edit modal and evaluates address presence, product matches, OOS status, delivery rate, and customer classification.

nodes.name: send_message_to_order
nodes.summary: Opens the messaging panel for an order row and sends a message with optional image attachments.

nodes.name: reply_comment_to_order
nodes.summary: Finds and replies to a Facebook comment on an order. Prioritises comments with 5+ consecutive asterisks.

nodes.name: save_product_images
nodes.summary: Downloads and compresses product images extracted from the order modal for later sending.

nodes.name: apply_campaign_filter
nodes.summary: Navigates the TPOS filter UI to select a campaign label and applies the filter.

nodes.name: rules.py
nodes.summary: Order classification and action decision logic. Classifies orders by address presence and pickup keywords, determines whether to skip/resend address requests, and selects the concrete action to perform.

nodes.name: classify_order
nodes.summary: Classifies an order as 'pickup', 'need_address', or 'has_address' based on note text keywords and address field content.

nodes.name: decide_action
nodes.summary: Returns a concrete action string for the current order state, combining classify_order result with resend cooldown logic.

nodes.name: should_skip
nodes.summary: Returns True if an existing order state has matching note/address hashes and is already in a terminal state.

nodes.name: should_resend_ask
nodes.summary: Checks if an 'asked_address' order is eligible for a resend based on a cooldown period since last action.

nodes.name: store.py
nodes.summary: State persistence and CSV export layer. Manages per-day JSON state files for order processing history, action audit CSV logging, order CSV export with incremental writer, and file path utilities.

nodes.name: OrderCsvWriter
nodes.summary: Context manager that incrementally writes enriched order rows to a CSV file with standard headers.

nodes.name: save_filtered_orders
nodes.summary: Writes a list of order dicts to a timestamped CSV file using atomic temp-file rename.

nodes.name: log_action
nodes.summary: Appends a timestamped action record to the per-day audit CSV log file.

nodes.name: upsert_order_state
nodes.summary: Loads per-day JSON state, updates the entry for an order code, and saves back.

nodes.name: text_hash
nodes.summary: Returns an MD5 hex digest of normalized text, or 'empty' for blank input.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init for the features module.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package init for the broadcast_order feature subpackage.

nodes.name: control.html
nodes.summary: Front-end control panel UI. Connects to the broadcast_order WebSocket server to receive live order feed updates. Renders a filterable order card feed with stats.

nodes.name: display.html
nodes.summary: Front-end display/broadcast UI. Connects to the broadcast_order WebSocket server to receive live order events. Renders a read-only live feed of order cards.

nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: Live feed broadcast server combining a static HTTP server for HTML files, a WebSocket server for real-time data push, and a periodic API fetch loop.

nodes.name: BroadcastServer
nodes.summary: Core server class managing WebSocket clients, periodic API fetch, tag persistence, Bearer token injection, and broadcasting payloads.

nodes.name: _StaticHandler
nodes.summary: BaseHTTPRequestHandler subclass serving static HTML files from the broadcast_order directory.

nodes.name: run_broadcast_server
nodes.summary: Public entry point that instantiates BroadcastServer and runs it via asyncio.run(), blocking until KeyboardInterrupt.

nodes.name: collect_order.py
nodes.summary: Implements the collect_order feature flow: reads all order rows, enriches them with tags/address/product data in a single pass, writes results to CSV.

nodes.name: run_collect_order_flow
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the collect-and-enrich single-pass flow: determines CSV path, delegates to OrderPage.collect_and_enrich_single_pass(), logs results.

nodes.name: confirm_order.py
nodes.summary: Implements the confirm_order feature: loads order codes from CSV, finds each order row via paginated search, performs edit actions, handles errors.

nodes.name: run_confirm_order_from_csv
nodes.summary: Iterates order codes loaded from CSV, locates each via paginated search, performs edit action, captures screenshot on failure.

nodes.name: load_order_codes_from_csv
nodes.summary: Reads and deduplicates order codes from a CSV file, supporting both 'Order_Code' and 'Order Code' column names.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Application entry point. Orchestrates feature selection via CLI, loads config, launches Playwright Chromium browser, handles authentication, applies campaign filter, and delegates to collect_order or confirm_order feature flows.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Main orchestrator: handles broadcast_order shortcut, prompts CLI inputs, loads config, launches Playwright browser, authenticates, navigates to orders page, applies campaign filter, and dispatches to collect or confirm workflows.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the runtime module; re-exports all public symbols from process_logger for convenient top-level access.

nodes.name: process_logger.py
nodes.summary: Provides timestamped console logging to stdout and daily log files, exception tracing with file append, stderr filtering, browser keep-open debug helper, and stdio flush utilities.

nodes.name: log_console
nodes.summary: Prints a timestamped message to stdout and appends it to a daily log file in data/logs/.

nodes.name: build_exception_logger
nodes.summary: Factory that returns a closure capturing error_dir and error_log_file, logging exceptions with full traceback.

nodes.name: keep_browser_open_for_debug
nodes.summary: Reads debug.keep_open from config; if enabled, waits for the configured duration before letting the browser close.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for workflows module; re-exports goto_orders from navigation.

nodes.name: navigation.py
nodes.summary: Provides goto_orders() which navigates the Playwright page to the ecommerce orders URL, waits for DOM load, then waits for the order table rows to become visible.

nodes.name: goto_orders
nodes.summary: Navigates Playwright page to the orders URL derived from config, waits for DOM load and order table row visibility.

nodes.name: test_campaign_cli.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for campaign date resolution and label building functions in app/cli_helpers.py.

nodes.name: CampaignCliTestCase
nodes.summary: TestCase class with 5 tests covering resolve_campaign_date and build_campaign_label.

nodes.name: test_config_loader.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for app/config_loader.py load_config function.

nodes.name: ConfigLoaderTestCase
nodes.summary: TestCase class with 2 tests: missing file raises FileNotFoundError, and partial YAML config merges correctly.

nodes.name: test_confirm_csv.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for features/confirm_order.py load_order_codes_from_csv function.

nodes.name: ConfirmCsvTestCase
nodes.summary: TestCase class with 2 tests for CSV order code loading: blank/duplicate filtering and header validation.

nodes.name: test_export_orders.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for app/store.py save_filtered_orders function.

nodes.name: ExportOrdersTestCase
nodes.summary: TestCase class with 1 test verifying save_filtered_orders writes correct CSV headers and row content.

nodes.name: test_note_parser.py
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit tests (540 lines) for app/note_parser.py extract_note_prices function.

nodes.name: TestExtractNotePrices
nodes.summary: TestCase class with 80+ test methods exhaustively testing extract_note_prices.

nodes.name: test_order_tagging.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for order tagging logic from app/order_page.py and tag constants from app/constants.py.

nodes.name: OrderTaggingTestCase
nodes.summary: TestCase class with 7 tests covering _resolve_product_match_tag for all address/count combinations.

nodes.name: test_rules.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for app/rules.py decide_action and should_resend_ask functions.

nodes.name: RulesTestCase
nodes.summary: TestCase class with 5 tests for decide_action covering pickup keyword, no-address, recently-asked skip, cooldown-expired resend, and address-present mark-done.

nodes.name: test_token_expiry.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for app/auth.py _is_saved_token_expired function.

nodes.name: TokenExpiryTestCase
nodes.summary: TestCase class with 3 tests: expired by unix timestamp, not expired, and expired by captured_at + expires_in_seconds.

nodes.name: test_token_parsing.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for app/auth.py _extract_bearer_from_value and _extract_token_metadata functions.

nodes.name: TokenParsingTestCase
nodes.summary: TestCase class with 2 tests verifying bearer token extraction and metadata extraction.

layers.name: Entry Point
layers.description: The single orchestration entry point that wires together CLI, config, Playwright browser lifecycle, authentication, and feature dispatch.

layers.name: Feature Layer
layers.description: Feature implementations for collect_order (enrichment + CSV export), confirm_order (batch confirmation), and broadcast_order (live WebSocket feed with HTML UIs).

layers.name: Core Application
layers.description: All app/* modules: the Page Object Model (order_page), auth primitives, config loading, business-rule classification, state persistence, CLI prompting, constants, note parsing.

layers.name: Runtime & Workflows
layers.description: Cross-cutting runtime infrastructure (process_logger) and navigation workflows (goto_orders) used by multiple features.

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: Unittest files covering rules, auth token handling, config loading, note parsing, order tagging, CSV export, and CLI helpers.

tour.title: Entry Point — main.py
tour.description: main.py is the single orchestration hub. It presents a CLI menu (feature selection, campaign date, CSV path), loads and validates config.yaml, launches a Playwright Chromium browser with session persistence, authenticates via saved token or auto/manual login, captures the Bearer token, navigates to the orders page, applies the campaign filter, and dispatches execution to either collect_order or confirm_order (or starts the broadcast server). All higher-level modules flow from this file.

tour.title: Config & CLI — bot_config.py, cli_helpers.py, cli_menu.py
tour.description: Configuration is the foundation of every run. config_loader.py reads config.yaml (with PyYAML + hand-rolled fallback) and normalises it into a canonical dict. BotConfig (bot_config.py) wraps that dict with typed properties and validates all required timeout and message keys at startup — a missing key is a hard error, never a silent default. The CLI layer (cli_helpers.py + cli_menu.py) uses InquirerPy to prompt the operator for feature, campaign date, A-code price mapping, and CSV file selection.

tour.title: Authentication — auth.py, login.py
tour.description: Authentication is a three-stage cascade. login.py's ensure_login() first tries to restore a saved Bearer token from disk (auth.py loads and checks expiry), seeding it into localStorage/sessionStorage/IndexedDB via Playwright evaluate. If that fails, try_auto_login() fills the login form using multiple selector fallbacks and submits. If both fail, the bot waits up to 300 s for manual login. Once authenticated, capture_and_save_auth_token() scans all browser storage locations for the JWT and saves it with expiry metadata for the next run.

tour.title: Order Page POM — order_page.py
tour.description: order_page.py is the largest file in the project (2310 lines) and the only module that directly drives the browser. The OrderPage class encapsulates every Playwright selector, pagination loop, modal interaction, product image download (PIL compression), message/image sending, Facebook comment reply, tag application, and campaign filter navigation. Two key workflows live here: read_filtered_orders() (first pass — collects row data without opening modals) and collect_and_enrich_single_pass() / enrich_collected_rows() (second pass — evaluates each order via _evaluate_modal_address_and_product, applies the tag, sends messages).

tour.title: Business Logic — rules.py, constants.py
tour.description: constants.py defines the complete tag taxonomy: TAG_0 (skip) through TAG_2_4 (no-address + OOS), plus helper sets RECHECK_TAGS, OOS_TAGS, and TAG_ONLY_TAGS. rules.py contains the two public decision functions: classify_order() maps note text + address field content to 'pickup' / 'need_address' / 'has_address', and decide_action() combines classification with a 24-hour cooldown check to return the concrete action string. note_parser.py provides the price-extraction primitive used during modal evaluation to match A-code prices.

tour.title: Collect Order Feature — features/collect_order.py, app/store.py
tour.description: collect_order.py implements the primary workflow: run_collect_order_flow() determines the output CSV path, then delegates to OrderPage.collect_and_enrich_single_pass() which processes all qualifying orders in a single browser pass. store.py provides the persistence layer: OrderCsvWriter is a context manager that incrementally flushes each enriched row to a timestamped CSV; upsert_order_state() maintains a per-day JSON file for deduplication across runs; log_action() appends every bot action to an audit CSV; text_hash() produces MD5 fingerprints for change detection.

tour.title: Confirm Order Feature — features/confirm_order.py
tour.description: confirm_order.py implements the batch-confirmation workflow. load_order_codes_from_csv() reads and deduplicates order codes from a previously exported CSV (supports both 'Order_Code' and 'Order Code' column names). run_confirm_order_from_csv() iterates each code, calls OrderPage.find_row_by_code_paginated() to locate it across pages, performs the edit action, and captures a screenshot on failure for later debugging.

tour.title: Broadcast Live Feed — features/broadcast_order/server.py, control.html, display.html
tour.description: The broadcast_order sub-package provides a real-time order monitoring feed independent of the Playwright bot. server.py starts two servers concurrently: a BaseHTTPServer serving the HTML files statically, and a websockets server pushing live order events to connected browser clients. BroadcastServer periodically fetches from the TPOS API (injecting the cached Bearer token), persists tagged orders to a local JSON file, and broadcasts payloads to all WebSocket clients. display.html renders a read-only animated feed (projector/second-screen use); control.html is an operator panel with filter buttons and tag/untag actions.

tour.title: Tests — coverage across all layers
tour.description: The test suite uses Python's built-in unittest (discoverable via 'python -m unittest discover -s tests'). Key files: test_rules.py verifies all decide_action paths (pickup, send_ask_address, skip, resend, mark_done); test_note_parser.py (540 lines, 80+ cases) exhaustively tests Vietnamese price extraction including A-code aliases, phone exclusion, and ambiguity handling; test_order_tagging.py tests the tag-assignment logic for all address/product-count combinations; test_token_expiry.py and test_token_parsing.py cover the auth token lifecycle; test_config_loader.py and test_campaign_cli.py cover config and CLI helpers.
